sections.heading: Current Focus
sections.content:
**V1 Stabilization** — Core features are built. Focus shifts to locking down the system, cleaning up, and proving it works through consumer-facing documentation.

Completed recently:
- ~~Viz TypeScript/ESM refactor (Tiers 1-3)~~ — Done (PEV cycle 2026-03-23)
- ~~ADR-006: JS/TS scanner via tree-sitter~~ — Done (PEV cycle 2026-03-24)
- ~~AST call graph validates edges~~ — Done
- ~~mark_clean baseline hash reset~~ — Done

sections.heading: V1 Scope
sections.content:
V1 includes three features. The indexer is the core; MCP server is the agent interface; viz dashboard is the developer observability layer.

### Indexer
The foundation. Scans Python and JS/TS code and DocJSON docs, builds a graph in SQLite, detects staleness, and gates PRs.

**Sub-features:**
- [x] **Scanning** — AST-based Python scanning, tree-sitter JS/TS scanning (ADR-006), DocJSON scanning, dFlow decorator detection, edge auto-generation, rename detection.
- [ ] **Staleness** — Mtime fast path, per-function hash comparison, CONTENT_UPDATED / DESC_UPDATED / LINKED_STALE / NOT_FOUND statuses, `cortex check`, `cortex mark-clean`. *Rename from old status names pending.*
- [x] **History** — Every upsert records a `node_history` row. Data feeds `cortex report`.
- [ ] ~~**Test Runner**~~ — *Deprecated. Removing from V1 scope.* `cortex test` was planned but never implemented. Test execution stays with pytest directly.
- [x] **Verification & Gate** — `node_verification` table, `cortex report`, mark-clean with baseline hash reset.

### MCP Server
- [x] **MCP Server** — Exposes cortex to AI agents via MCP protocol (stdio transport). Core tools implemented: build, render, list, graph, search, check, history, mark-clean, write-doc, update-section, add-section, add-link, read-doc, checkout, sql, report, list-undocumented, list-reference-points, diff, source. *Pending: delete tools (delete_section, delete_link, delete_doc), list_tags, tag filter on search, move parameters on update_section.*

### Viz Dashboard
Developer observability layer. Fully implemented as TypeScript ES modules (refactored 2026-03-23).

**Sub-features:**
- [x] **Graph View** — Interactive Cytoscape.js node graph with type coloring and staleness rings.
- [x] **List View** — Filterable, sortable node table.
- [x] **Detail Panel** — Node metadata drawer (docstring, history, test results, dFlow steps).
- [x] **Test Dashboard** — Test explorer with tier badges, JUnit results, validates edges.
- [x] **Doc Manager** — DocJSON viewer/editor with section editing, link picker, Mermaid diagrams.
- [x] **Workflow View** — dFlow workflow/task explorer with step drill-down and Monaco source.
- [x] **Cross-Doc View** — Cross-document section view.

sections.heading: Next Up
sections.content:
See `cortex::docs.releases.v1` for the complete V1 release checklist — bugs, improvements, code changes, audits, and E2E acceptance stories.

High-level order:
1. Staleness rename (terminology foundation)
2. Bug fixes (viz stabilization)
3. MCP tool gaps
4. Audits (tags, tests, docs alignment)
5. Consumer documentation site (capstone)
6. E2E acceptance stories → T3 tests
